chunk:
CHUNK:012/021
She had to be to unsnap my holster without me feeling it, and to draw my pistol \u2013 backwards \u2013 and turn it in her hands before I had a chance to react. My stomach flipped as I stared into the barrel of my gun and wondered what Gaba would say when he found out what had happened. Even then, at gunpoint, I almost laughed at my priorities. I was less concerned about what Gaba would do than the verbal reprimand I expected to receive, followed by another from my supervisor, Sergeant Duneq, when Gaba gave him the good news. \u201cOkay, honey,\u201d Rachael said as she waved the gun in my face. \u201cI don\u2019t know what just happened, but it looks like you had a whole conversation with yourself in about two seconds.\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. \u201cBut you\u2019re okay?\u201d \u201cI don\u2019t know,\u201d I said. \u201cAm I?\u201d \u201cSure.\u201d Rachael nodded. \u201cI\u2019m not going to hurt anything more than your pride, honey. But I am going to need that big fancy gun of yours. So, if you don\u2019t mind, you\u2019re going to unsnap that big boy from the sling and hand it over.\u201d Rachael waved the gun again, adding, \u201cSlowly. Okay?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. Rachael kept my gun steady as she pointed it at my face. I unclipped the MP5 and slowly handed it over. It did feel a little lighter than usual, but I was too busy disarming myself to give it much thought. Rachael took the submachine gun and immediately slipped my pistol into the loose pocket of her housecoat to get a better grip on the MP5. \u201cNow who\u2019s outgunned?\u201d she said with a grin. I sighed and said, \u201cThat would be me. But have you thought this through?\u201d \u201cHonestly, honey, I don\u2019t often think further than my next cappuccino. But seeing as you asked, I can tell you.\u201d She took a breath, shifted her stance, and I waited. The midnight sun dipped behind a distant mountain peak to cast a short-lived shadow on the starboard side of Dreamcaster. I guessed that Rachael had been in Greenland long enough to know the sun was on her side, and whatever she needed to get done could be accomplished in daylight. But Rachael seemed too busy thinking about just how much she intended to tell me about her plans to notice, and I leaned back in my chair, sensing no immediate threat to my life, just a warning. \u201cOkay,\u201d Rachael said with a nod, as if she had made a decision. \u201cHere\u2019s the plan, Petra. You ready?\u201d \u201cI am,\u201d I said. \u201cOkay then. That\u2019s good. Because you\u2019re a big part of making this plan work. So\u2026\u201d Rachael took a breath and then glanced at the door, as if she was suddenly aware that it might burst open at any minute. She adjusted her position and moved to put me between herself and Gaba if he was to breach the cabin. \u201cWe have some errands,\u201d she said. \u201cSuch as?\u201d \u201cJust errands, honey. Things we need to get done.\u201d \u201cYou\u2019re not planning on escaping, then?\u201d Rachael laughed and said, \u201cYou told me yourself, Petra. There\u2019s nowhere to go. Even if I got as far as the airport, it\u2019s not like there\u2019s a plane sitting on the runway just ready to go. So, no, I\u2019m not planning to escape. But there are things I need to get done, and you\u2019re going to help me.\u201d \u201cOkay,\u201d I said. I moved as if I was about to stand. \u201cNot so fast,\u201d Rachael said. She waggled the MP5 in front of me, and I sank back into the chair. \u201cYou\u2019re awfully cooperative, Officer. And, as you might imagine, that makes me nervous.\u201d \u201cYes, I can imagine.\u201d \u201cSo, either you\u2019re planning something, or you\u2019ve taken a page out of the what-to-do-when-I\u2019m-taken-hostage-by-a-criminal playbook. I couldn\u2019t say which one it is, but if this is the cooperating stage of your how-to manual, then I\u2019ll accept that, and trust that you will cooperate and sit there like a good girl, while I pull some pants on. Okay?\u201d \u201cI can wait,\u201d I said, thinking it would be a lot easier for Gaba and the SRU to overpower Rachael in the corridor than in her cabin. \u201cYou can wait\u2026\u201d Rachael shook her head. \u201cCool as the proverbial cucumber, eh?\u201d \u201cI try,\u201d I said, as Rachael backed around the bed to the closet. She held the MP5 in one hand \u2013 still pointed at me \u2013 and tugged a pair of jeans out of the closet, along with a sweater. I didn\u2019t know how she intended to dress using just one hand, but she managed her jeans, and then with an embarrassed shrug, asked if I wouldn\u2019t mind lying on the floor while she took her housecoat off and pulled on her sweater. I didn\u2019t mind. I lay on the floor. Rachael cursed when my pistol fell out of the pocket of her housecoat, but I kept my face pressed into the carpet and wondered if it might have been better to turn the television off after all. \u201cStay down, Petra,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cI\u2019m just getting my boots on, okay?\u201d \u201cTake as long as you need,\u201d I said. I slipped my hand to my side and pressed my fingers against the cover of Paniula\u2019s diary, sighing when I confirmed it had not fallen out. Thoughts of reading it were just going to have to wait, but the twist of fate that put me at the mercy of Rachael Silk had the same effect as the diary and about ten cups of coffee. I was awake. \u201cRight,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cI\u2019m ready if you are, Petra?\u201d \u201cI\u2019m ready,\u201d I said. \u201cDo you want me to stand up?\u201d \u201cSure. Go ahead. But slowly, okay?\u201d I stood up \u2013 slowly \u2013 and turned around to see a very different Rachael, complete with hiking boots with thick socks turned down over the cuffs, dark jeans, and a thick fleece sweater.
compressed_context:
CTX: CABIN-OF-DREAMCASTER; LOCATION: GREENLAND; TIME: MIDNIGHT-SUN

PAST: RACHAEL-SILK SILENTLY UNSNAPS PETRA-HOLSTER ∧ DRAWS PETRA-PISTOL BACKWARDS.

PAST: RACHAEL-SILK TURNS PETRA-PISTOL BEFORE PETRA-REACTS.

PETRA: STOMACH-FLIP ∧ STARES INTO BARREL ∧ WONDERS GABA-REACTION.

PRIORITY: VERBAL-REPRIMAND(GABA+DUNEQ) > PERSONAL-SAFETY.

RACHAEL-SILK: "OKAY, HONEY, YOU JUST HAD A TWO-SECOND CONVERSATION WITH YOURSELF."

PETRA: "YES. YOU OKAY?"

RACHAEL-SILK: "I DON’T KNOW. AM I?"

PETRA: "SURE."

RACHAEL-SILK: "I WON’T HURT MORE THAN YOUR PRIDE. HAND OVER YOUR MP5 SLOWLY."

PAST: PETRA UNSNAPS MP5-SLING ∧ HANDS IT OVER SLOWLY.

RACHAEL-SILK: TRANSFERS PETRA-PISTOL→HOUSECOAT-POCKET ∧ GRIPS MP5.

RACHAEL-SILK: "NOW WHO’S OUTGUNNED?"

PETRA: SIGHS "ME. BUT YOU THOUGHT THIS THROUGH?"

RACHAEL-SILK: "I OFTEN THINK NO FURTHER THAN MY NEXT CAPPUCCINO. PLAN FOLLOWS."

PAST: MIDNIGHT-SUN DIPS BEHIND MOUNTAIN ∧ SHADOW ON DREAMCASTER-STARBOARD.

PETRA: NOTES RACHAEL’S INTENT FOCUS.

RACHAEL-SILK: "HERE’S THE PLAN, PETRA. YOU READY?"

PETRA: "I AM."

RACHAEL-SILK: GLANCES AT DOOR ∧ REPOS PETRA BETWEEN SELF ∧ GABA-BREACH.

RACHAEL-SILK: "WE HAVE ERRANDS."

PETRA: "SUCH AS?"

RACHAEL-SILK: "JUST ERRANDS."

PETRA: "NOT PLANNING ESCAPE?"

RACHAEL-SILK: "NOWHERE TO GO. NO PLANE. BUT YOU’LL HELP ME."

PETRA: "OKAY."

PAST: PETRA BEGINS TO STAND.

RACHAEL-SILK: WAGGLES MP5 ∧ PETRA SINKS BACK.

RACHAEL-SILK: "YOU’RE COOPERATIVE. I’M NERVOUS."

PETRA: "I TRY."

RACHAEL-SILK: "EITHER YOU HAVE A PLAN OR USE HOSTAGE-PLAYBOOK. IF COOPERATING-STAGE, SIT LIKE GOOD-GIRL WHILE I DRESS."

PAST: PETRA LAYS FACE-INTO CARPET.

RACHAEL-SILK: PULLS JEANS+SWEATER ONE-HAND.

PAST: PETRA-PISTOL FALLS FROM HOUSECOAT-POCKET ∧ RACHAEL-SILK CURSES.

PETRA: HAND ON DIARY-COVER IN POCKET ∧ CONFIRMS DIARY-INTACT.

PETRA: AWAKE LIKE AFTER 10 CUPS-OF-COFFEE.

RACHAEL-SILK: "I’M READY IF YOU ARE, PETRA."

PETRA: "I’M READY. STAND UP?"

RACHAEL-SILK: "SURE. SLOWLY."

PAST: PETRA STANDS SLOWLY ∧ TURNS.

PAST: PETRA SEES RACHAEL IN HIKING-BOOTS ∧ JEANS ∧ FLEECE-SWEATER.
